title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
slides: World leaders want American AI, but fear a kill switch.
Sovereign AI clouds keep control local, not in Washington.
One nation just built a model that rivals GPT—without US chips.
Your data now stays inside borders; no foreign policy can touch it.
Audit your AI vendor's sovereignty clause before your next update.
thread_text:
1. Who owns the kill switch?

One nation just built a GPT rival without American chips — and kept its data out of Washington’s reach.

2. Sovereign clouds bypass US control.

Local infrastructure keeps your data beyond foreign policy reach. No export law can touch it.

3. The GPU workaround is real.

That rival model was trained on non-US hardware. The silicon ban just lost its teeth.

4. Your data stays behind borders.

Sovereign AI clouds enforce local storage by law. Even a presidential order can’t pull your logs.

5. Check your vendor’s sovereignty clause.

Before your next update, audit where inference happens. Does your contract let data leave without consent?
video_script: Leaders want American AI but fear control.
Sovereign clouds keep data out of Washington.
One nation built a GPT rival without US chips.
Your data now stays inside your borders.
Audit your vendor's sovereignty clause now.

script: Leaders want American AI but fear control.
Sovereign clouds keep data out of Washington.
One nation built a GPT rival without US chips.
Your data now stays inside your borders.
Audit your vendor's sovereignty clause now.

instagram.caption:
A nation just built a GPT-level rival without American chips—and its data stays locked in-country, beyond Washington's reach.

World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to hold the kill switch. Sole sovereign clouds now enforce local storage by law; no foreign policy or export ban can touch your logs. The silicon ban lost its teeth.

For you: every future tool you build could run on hardware your government can’t block—or data it can’t subpoena.

Before your next vendor update: does your contract let inference data leave your border without your consent?
